info.name: Profile – Delete Account (DELETE /api/v1/profile/me + POST /api/v1/profile/me/cancel-deletion)

exec: // Increment the request sequence counter and derive a unique X-Forwarded-For IP
// for every request so non-RL folders never exhaust any rate-limit bucket.
// The rate-limiting folders override _xff to 127.0.0.1 in their own prerequest.

item.name: setup
item.description:
Registers del_user_a through del_user_d plus +delvc alias. Fetches verification OTPs from Gmail, verifies all accounts, and logs in.

del_user_a  : gmail_test_email         — Path A happy-path, cancel, failures/wrong-password, auth-failures, validation/cancel.
del_user_b  : gmail_test_email+delb   — happy-path-email-otp (slots 1-2), telegram-guards/stale-auth (slot 3, last).
del_user_c  : gmail_test_email+delvc  — failures/empty-body (slot 1), validation/malformed-JSON (slot 2).
del_user_d  : gmail_test_email+delvd  — telegram-guards/id=0 (slot 1), validation/body>1MiB (slot 2).

exec: console.error('[ERROR] gmail_test_email not set in environment — add it to environment.json.');
// --- Gmail verification OTP fetch for del_user_a ---
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for del_user_a verification email to arrive in Gmail...');
console.log('[INFO] Done waiting. Querying Gmail API...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + delEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired — refresh at https://developers.google.com/oauthplayground'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No matching verification email found for del_user_a.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode del_user_a verification email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No 6-digit OTP found in del_user_a verification email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] del_user_a verification OTP stored in del_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] del_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in register step did not complete.');
console.error('        Check: gmail_access_token is set, token not expired, --delay-request >= 8000.');
console.log('[INFO] del_user_a access token stored in del_access_token.');
// Derive the +delb Gmail alias from gmail_test_email.
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for del_user_b (+delb) verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + bEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +delb verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +delb email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +delb email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] del_user_b OTP stored in del_b_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] del_b_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in +delb register step did not complete.');
console.log('[INFO] del_user_b access token stored in del_b_access_token.');
// Derive the +delvc Gmail alias from gmail_test_email.
// Used exclusively for validation/DELETE /me malformed JSON (fresh del:usr: bucket).
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for del_user_c (+delvc) verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + cEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +delvc verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +delvc email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +delvc email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] del_user_c OTP stored in del_c_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] del_c_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in +delvc register step did not complete.');
console.log('[INFO] del_user_c access token stored in del_c_access_token.');
if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for del_user_d (+delvd) verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + dEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +delvd verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +delvd email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +delvd email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] del_user_d OTP stored in del_d_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.error('[ERROR] del_d_vfy_code is empty — Gmail fetch in +delvd register step did not complete.');
console.log('[INFO] del_user_d access token stored in del_d_access_token.');

item.name: happy-path-password
item.description:
Path A (T-01, T-20, T-34): del_user_a has has_password=true.
DELETE /me with password → 200 schedules deletion.
GET /me confirms JWT is still valid (D-02: session NOT revoked).
POST /auth/login confirms pending-deletion accounts can still log in (D-04 / T-34).
Repeat DELETE while deletion is already pending → 409 already_pending_deletion (T-20).

exec: pm.test('message is account scheduled for deletion', () => {
pm.test('scheduled_deletion_at present and parseable as RFC3339', () => {

item.name: GET /me → 200 (D-02: session NOT revoked after soft-delete)

exec: pm.test('scheduled_deletion_at matches value from DELETE response', () => {

item.name: POST /auth/login → 200 (D-04 / T-34: pending-deletion account can still log in)

exec: // D-04 / T-34: After soft-delete, login must still succeed.
// T-34 soft check: scheduled_deletion_at in login response (not yet implemented)

item.name: happy-path-cancel
item.description:
T-27: del_user_a has deleted_at set from happy-path-password.
POST /cancel-deletion → 200 clears deleted_at.
GET /me confirms scheduled_deletion_at is absent (omitempty).
Repeat POST /cancel-deletion → 409 not_pending_deletion.

item.name: happy-path-deletion-method
item.description:
Wire-checks for GET /api/v1/profile/me/deletion-method using del_user_a (password user).
The endpoint has no side-effects; it never issues OTPs or modifies state.

exec: pm.test('409 already_pending_deletion when account is pending', function() {

item.name: telegram-guards
item.description:
T-14 / T-15: del_b_access_token for T-15 (stale auth_date — del_b slot 3, last allowed).
del_d_access_token for T-14 (id=0 — del_b bucket exhausted at this point; del_d slot 1).

item.name: DELETE /me {telegram_auth stale auth_date} → 401 (invalid_telegram_auth — T-15 replay guard)
failures
item.description:
Domain error paths for DELETE /me.
del_user_a (del_access_token): wrong-password → slot 3, last allowed (slots 1+2 used by happy-path-password).
del_user_c (del_c_access_token): empty-body → del_c slot 1 (del_user_a bucket exhausted at this point).

exec: pm.test('code is validation_error (empty body, password user)', () => {

item.name: auth-failures
item.description:
JWT auth middleware guards for DELETE /me and POST /cancel-deletion.
No valid token — requests are rejected before rate limiter or handler.

item.name: validation
item.description:
Body-size and malformed-JSON cases.
DELETE /me body>1MiB uses del_d_access_token (del_d slot 1 → 413; del_b bucket exhausted after telegram-guards).
DELETE /me malformed JSON uses del_c_access_token (del_c slot 2 → 400; slot 1 consumed by failures/empty-body).
POST cancel-deletion cases use del_access_token (auth middleware fires before del:usr: limiter).

exec: pm.test('status is 413 (body too large for DELETE /me)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(413));
pm.test('status is 400 (malformed JSON for DELETE /me)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));
pm.test('status is 413 (body too large for POST /cancel-deletion)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(413));
pm.test('status is 400 (malformed JSON for POST /cancel-deletion)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(400));

item.name: rate-limiting-del
item.description:
Verifies del:usr: (10 req / 1hr per user) fires correctly for DELETE /me.
Uses a dedicated +delrl user with a fresh bucket.

exec: if (!token) { console.warn('[WARN] gmail_access_token not set — skipping Gmail fetch'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] Waiting 8 s for +delrl verification email...');
const query = encodeURIComponent('subject:"Verify your" to:' + rlEmail + ' newer_than:1h');
if (err) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail list failed:', err); return; }
if (res.code === 401) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail token expired'); return; }
if (!msgs || !msgs.length) { console.warn('[WARN] No +delrl verification email found.'); return; }
if (err2) { console.error('[ERROR] Gmail fetch failed:', err2); return; }
const p = parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/plain') || parts.find(x => x.mimeType === 'text/html');
if (!body) { console.warn('[WARN] Could not decode +delrl email body.'); return; }
if (!match) { console.warn('[WARN] No OTP found in +delrl email.'); return; }
console.log('[INFO] +delrl OTP stored in delrl_vfy_code:', match[1]);
console.log('[INFO] +delrl access token stored in delrl_access_token.');
pm.test('status is 200 (del:usr: slot 10/10 — last allowed)', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));

item.description:

Uses a dedicated +cncrl user with a fresh bucket.

item.name: prime 1 — DELETE /me → accept 200 or 429 (prime for cancel)
warmup 10/10 — POST /me/cancel-deletion → accept 200 or 409 (delc:usr: slot 10, last allowed)

exec: pm.test('not rate-limited yet (delc:usr: slot 10 — last allowed)', () => pm.expect(pm.response.code).to.not.eql(429));

item.name: deletion-method
item.description:
Wire-checks for GET /api/v1/profile/me/deletion-method.

All checks reuse del_user_a (password account) — no additional aliases needed.
The endpoint has no side-effects; it never issues OTPs or modifies state.

variable.description: Auto-incremented counter for unique XFF IPs.
X-Forwarded-For value. Set per-request by collection prerequest; pinned to 127.0.0.1 by RL folders.
JWT for del_user_b. Used for happy-path-email-otp and telegram-guards.
gmail_test_email+delvc — del_user_c. Fresh del:usr: bucket for validation/malformed-JSON.
JWT for del_user_c. Used for failures/empty-body and validation/malformed-JSON.
gmail_test_email+delvd — del_user_d. Fresh del:usr: bucket for telegram-guards/id=0 and validation/body>1MiB.
JWT for del_user_d. Used for telegram-guards/id=0 and validation/body>1MiB.
gmail_test_email+delrl — fresh del:usr: bucket for rate-limiting-del.
gmail_test_email+cncrl — fresh delc:usr: bucket for rate-limiting-delc.
